assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The foreign exchange exposure relates to an intragroup dividend receivable/payable that has been recognised as a monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities and components of such items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including relevant foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 context includes hedge accounting for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model.
